summary:
Claim: The AP view of a lumbar spine X-ray is the least useful for diagnosing spondylolisthesis.  
Evidence: - AP views suffer from structural superimposition, blurring vertebral alignment and hiding slippage. - Lateral views preserve true axial orientation, enabling accurate measurement of posterior-to-anterior displacement.  
Answer: C
